title: As AI Investments Surge, CEOs Take the Lead
executive_summary: 73% of CEOs now identify themselves as their organisation's primary AI decision-maker — double the share from a year ago — marking a fundamental shift from delegating AI to the CTO or CIO
Corporate AI spending is expected to double in 2026, rising from 0.8% to 1.7% of revenues, with 90% of companies planning to sustain or increase investment regardless of near-term returns

BCG's analysis of 2,400 executives across 16 markets identifies three CEO archetypes: 15% Trailblazers (systematic, workforce-first), 70% Pragmatists (ROI-focused, selective), and 15% Followers (reactive, risk-averse)

Trailblazers distinguish themselves by treating AI as an organisational redesign challenge — investing simultaneously in strategy, talent, culture, and risk governance rather than deploying point solutions
the_one_insight: CEOs have stopped asking whether AI is worth investing in — 90% will increase or hold spend regardless of near-term returns. The strategic question has moved from ROI justification to organisational redesign: who owns it, who is upskilled, and how fast workflows are rebuilt around agents.
